academic_requirements.summary: Applicants need an upper secondary school diploma or equivalent foreign qualification suitable for university access and must qualify through the national ministerial admission procedure for the English-taught Medicine and Surgery programme.
academic_requirements.prior_degree_or_diploma: Secondary Studies diploma (or equivalent). Applicants with foreign qualifications must also meet the University of Padua country-based entry-title requirements.
academic_requirements.subject_requirements: Admission is based on the English-language IMAT and on the ministerial selection rules for the programme. The detailed 2026/27 ministerial call had not yet been published as of 2026-06-04 [uncertain].
academic_requirements.gpa_or_country_requirements: No GPA threshold is stated on the current official programme and admissions pages. Country-based specific entry-title requirements apply to foreign qualifications.

language_requirements.required_level: No separate English language certificate is required for applicants sitting IMAT; the current official language-requirement page exempts IMAT candidates from presenting a certificate. The current Medicine-specific pages do not state a separate CEFR level for 2026/27 [uncertain].
accepted_proofs: IMAT participation is accepted as an exemption from presenting a separate English language certificate.

Under the University's central English-language page, accepted certificates include TOEFL iBT, IELTS Academic or General Training, Cambridge ESOL, Pearson, Oxford Test of English, and Trinity College London certificates.
exemption_rules: Applicants sitting IMAT do not need to present an English language proficiency certificate.
Applicants who completed their previous education entirely in English are exempt.
Native English speakers from the countries listed by the University of Padua are exempt.
language_requirements.deadline: No separate certificate deadline is stated for IMAT candidates on the current Medicine-specific pages; the 2026/27 call had not yet been published as of 2026-06-04 [uncertain].

application_deadline_eu.notes: The official 2026/27 Medicine and Surgery call had not yet been published as of 2026-06-04. The current official admissions page says application dates, application procedure, and test dates will be published in the annual call around June/July [uncertain].

application_deadline_non_eu.notes: The official 2026/27 Medicine and Surgery call had not yet been published as of 2026-06-04. Non-EU candidates residing outside Italy must also pre-enrol through Universitaly once the call opens [uncertain].

required_documents.document_name: Upper secondary school diploma or equivalent
required_documents.notes: The Medicine and Surgery course factsheet states 'Secondary Studies diploma (or equivalent)' as the entry qualification.

The central application page for English-taught bachelor's and single-cycle programmes lists the transcript of records among the upload documents for applicants with international entry qualifications.

required_documents.document_name: Certificate proving 1 or 2 years of academic studies, or a post-secondary qualification, or a foundation course qualification
required_documents.notes: Required only if the applicant does not have a minimum 12-year school record.

required_documents.document_name: Certificate of entrance examination to access university
required_documents.notes: Required only if the law of the country where the upper secondary certificate was awarded requires such an examination.

required_documents.document_name: Official translation into English or Italian of academic documents
required_documents.notes: Required if the educational documents are not already issued in English or Italian.
The central page specifies a valid ID card for EU citizens and non-EU citizens residing in Italy, or a passport.
Required only for non-EU citizens residing in Italy.

required_documents.document_name: Universitaly registration and, for non-EU applicants residing abroad, Universitaly pre-enrolment
required_documents.notes: Registration on Universitaly is required to apply to Medicine and Surgery. Non-EU citizens residing outside Italy must also pre-enrol through Universitaly.

required_documents.document_name: Original and legalised copies of previous qualifications
required_documents.notes: The Medicine and Surgery course factsheet states that no legalised documents are required at application stage, but admitted students will later have to submit original and translated/legalised copies of their previous qualifications.

entry_exam_or_test.applies_to: All applicants to the English-taught Medicine and Surgery programme.
entry_exam_or_test.dates: If the admission procedures are confirmed by the Ministry compared to previous years, the admission test is expected to be held during the third week of September 2026. The official 2026/27 date was not yet published as of 2026-06-04 [uncertain].
entry_exam_or_test.scoring_or_threshold: Candidates proceed based on places available, their ranking, and whether they achieved the minimum score set by the admission test. The 2026/27 threshold had not yet been published as of 2026-06-04 [uncertain].

source_quotes.quote: Candidates must pass an admission test in English (IMAT)
You are sitting the IMAT, the mandatory entry test
the admission test is expected to be held during the third week of September

uncertainty_notes: As of 2026-06-04, the official 2026/27 ministerial call and decree for Medicine and Surgery had not yet been published; the university instructs candidates to wait for the annual call around June/July.
Current official sources clearly confirm IMAT-based admission and Universitaly registration, but they do not yet publish the final 2026/27 test date, ranking threshold, or application windows.
The University of Padua website notes that only the official Italian version has legal validity where translated pages differ.
